Harold & Kumar Escape from Guantanamo Bay (2008)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $38,108,728Details
International Box Office $5,244,389
Worldwide Box Office $43,353,117
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $25,229,573
Total $25,229,573
Further financial details...

Synopsis

After the high-flying stoners try to smuggle a bong aboard a flight to Amsterdam, the two find themselves in jail on charges of terrorism. After making an amazing escape, Harold and Kumar flee to Texas by way of Florida, where they hope to convince Harold’s well-connected friend to help them out of their predicament. The problem is, Harold’s pal is about to marry Kumar’s ex-sweetie.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$14,908,404 (39.1% of total gross)
Legs:2.56 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:87.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$12,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,510 opening theaters/2,545 max. theaters, 4.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $63,479,159

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 25th, 2008 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
Video Release: July 29th, 2008 by New Line Home Video
MPA Rating: R for strong crude and sexual content, graphic nudity, pervasive language and drug use.
Running Time: 102 minutes
Franchise: Harold & Kumar
Cast, crew, or production detail: Epilogue, Fictionalized Version of Yourself
Plot point: Road Trip, Terrorism
Subgenre: Stoner Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: New Line Cinema, Mandate Pictures, Kingsgate Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

After the high-flying stoners try to smuggle a bong aboard a flight to Amsterdam, the two find themselves in jail on charges of terrorism. After making an amazing escape, Harold and Kumar flee to Texas by way of Florida, where they hope to convince Harold’s well-connected friend to help them out of their predicament. The problem is, Harold’s pal is about to marry Kumar’s ex-sweetie.
